_doc: Hand-built faithful-replay fixtures for a UiPath.WorkflowEvents.Activities InitializeHubConnection AggregateException. Signatures and stack frames are source-grounded (InitializeHubConnection.ExecuteAsync throws WorkflowApplicationException 'SignalR: Invalid SessionId: ... OR Orchestrator Url: ...' synchronously; the async-activity framework surfaces it as AggregateException). Substring dispatch, first match wins.

rules._doc: Error-level log row repeats the exception already visible in jobs get.
The Error-level set answers any logs variant.
Pending -> Running -> Faulted; the activity fails fast in its synchronous bootstrap (~3s), confirming it is not a transport/connect wait.

Two spans: RobotJob (AggregateException wrapping WorkflowApplicationException) and the Initialize Apps Hub Connection span (FullTypeName UiPath.WorkflowEvents.Activities.NetCore.Activities.InitializeHubConnection, SessionId='' — the blank session id that triggers the synchronous throw). The blank SessionId + the inner 'Invalid SessionId OR Orchestrator Url' message are the cause-disambiguation evidence (session/URL bootstrap vs SignalR socket failure).
Alternate trace-retrieval form; same span payload.
Positional TraceId form; same span payload.
Substring dispatch routes --state Faulted variants here too; the failing job is the most recent row.
docsai queries always passthrough — query strings vary between runs.

unmocked_default._doc: Permissive fallback: any unmatched uip command returns an empty array.

expected_calls.description: Triage must resolve the Shared folder key before listing jobs.
Agent must list jobs in folder Shared to identify the most recent (faulted) job.

Agent must fetch the failing job to read the System.AggregateException wrapping System.Activities.WorkflowApplicationException 'SignalR: Invalid SessionId:  OR Orchestrator Url: ' from UiPath.WorkflowEvents.Activities.NetCore.Activities.InitializeHubConnection.

Cause disambiguation: the Initialize Apps Hub Connection span shows SessionId='' (blank), pinning the fault to the synchronous session/URL bootstrap rather than a SignalR socket/transport failure (which the activity swallows on a background thread).
OPTIONAL — Error-level log repeats the exception already visible in jobs get.
OPTIONAL — fast (~3s) Pending -> Running -> Faulted confirms a synchronous bootstrap failure, not a connect wait.
